ddls:
CREATE TABLE "episodes" (
  "episode_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each episode */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the episode */,
  "show_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the show the episode belongs to */,
  "season_number" INTEGER /* Season number of the episode */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the episode */,
  "episode_runtime" INTEGER /* Duration of the episode in minutes */,
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the director of the episode */,
  "writer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the writer of the episode */,
  "production_company" TEXT /* Name of the production company that produced the episode */,
  "syndication_status" TEXT /* Whether the episode is syndicated or not */,
  "view_count" INTEGER /* Total number of views for the episode */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Revenue generated from the episode */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("episode_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_episodes_writer_id FOREIGN KEY ("writer_id") REFERENCES writers ("writer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_episodes_show_id FOREIGN KEY ("show_id") REFERENCES shows ("show_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_episodes_director_id FOREIGN KEY ("director_id") REFERENCES directors ("director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shows" (
  "show_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each show */,
  "show_name" TEXT /* Name of the show */,
  "genre" TEXT /* Genre of the show (e.g., Comedy, Drama) */,
  "network" TEXT /* Network that airs the show */,
  "producer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the producer of the show */,
  "show_runtime" INTEGER /* Average runtime of episodes in the show */,
  "country_of_origin" TEXT /* Country where the show was produced */,
  "language" TEXT /* Primary language of the show */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the show first aired */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the show ended (if applicable) */,
  "number_of_episodes" INTEGER /* Total number of episodes in the show */,
  "average_rating" REAL /* Average rating of all episodes in the show */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("show_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shows_producer_id FOREIGN KEY ("producer_id") REFERENCES producers ("producer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating entry */,
  "episode_id" INTEGER /* ID of the episode being rated */,
  "rating_value" REAL /* Rating value given by the user (1 to 10 scale) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who submitted the rating */,
  "rating_date" TEXT /* Date when the rating was submitted */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Optional comment provided by the user when rating the episode */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used to submit the rating */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user when submitting the rating */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_episode_id FOREIGN KEY ("episode_id") REFERENCES episodes ("episode_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES user_profiles ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_profiles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., viewer, admin) */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the user */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the user */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the user */,
  "favorite_genres" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of favorite genres */,
  "subscription_type" TEXT /* Type of subscription */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_profiles_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "engagement_metrics" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement entry */,
  "episode_id" INTEGER /* ID of the episode for the engagement metrics */,
  "PC1" REAL /* Principal component score 1 */,
  "PC2" REAL /* Principal component score 2 */,
  "PC3" REAL /* Principal component score 3 */,
  "social_media_score" REAL /* Score derived from social media sentiment analysis */,
  "advertising_effectiveness" REAL /* Score indicating the effectiveness of advertising during the episode */,
  "demographics" TEXT /* Demographic information of the audience */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_engagement_metrics_episode_id FOREIGN KEY ("episode_id") REFERENCES episodes ("episode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_engagement" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user engaging with the episode */,
  "episode_id" INTEGER /* ID of the episode being engaged with */,
  "view_time" REAL /* Total view time in minutes */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the episode was accessed */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used to view the episode */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user when accessing the episode */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_engagement_episode_id FOREIGN KEY ("episode_id") REFERENCES episodes ("episode_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_engagement_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES user_profiles ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the role */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "directors" (
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each director */,
  "director_name" TEXT /* Name of the director */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the director */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the director */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "writers" (
  "writer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each writer */,
  "writer_name" TEXT /* Name of the writer */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the writer */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the writer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("writer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "producers" (
  "producer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each producer */,
  "producer_name" TEXT /* Name of the producer */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the producer */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the producer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("producer_id")
)
